rulings.Case Title: EVERETT TAUBER VS. RICHARD Y. LEE et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 1, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANTS RICHARD LEE, JEAN CHIN AND JOAN ONG'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT'S STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED FACTS FAILS TO LIST FACTS FOR ISSUES THREE, FIVE AND SEVEN. FOR ISSUE ONE, THE STATEMENT IS SILENT AS TO ANY FACTS SUPPORTING THE CONTENTION THAT DEFENDANT IS ENGAGED IN A LAWFUL BUSINESS OR IS NOT ENGAGED IN UNFAIR PRACTICES. WITH RESPECT TO ISSUES TWO, FOUR AND SIX, DEFENDANT'S RELIANCE ON THE TESTIMONY OF PLAINTIFF IS INADEQUATE BASED ON THE HOLDING IN WEBER V JOHN CRANE, INC. (2006) 143 CAL. APP. 4TH 1433. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE MORALES et al VS. CLUB ONE, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Re-Notice) Of Hearing On X-Deft Michelle Morales' Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs Following Order (Ccp 425.16)
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 1, 2008 LINE 8. PLAINTIFF MICHELLE MORALES'(Re-Notice) CROSS-DEFENDANT Michelle Morales' Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs Following Order (C.C.P. SECTION 425.16): THE HEARING IS CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 14, 2008 TO BE HEARD IN CONJUNCTION WITH DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION. GIVEN THAT AN AWARD OF FEES IS MANDATORY, IT IS ONLY PRUDENT TO FIX THE AMOUNT WHEN THIS MATTER IS FINALLY DETERMINED IN THE TRIAL COURT. THE COURT, ON ITS' OWN MOTION, IS CONTINUING THE MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION FROM FEBRUARY 13, 2008 TO FEBRUARY 14, 2008. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: MOLLY PETTIT VS. ANITA MARGRILL et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 1, 2008 LINE 9. DEFENDANT ANITA MARGRILL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR, NO PROOF OF SERVICE. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: AL SHAWA VS. JACK TAM et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 1, 2008 LINE 10. CROSS-DEFENDANT AL SHAWA'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT: DEMURRER OF SHAWA IS OVERRULED, TEN DAYS TO ANSWER. A WRITTEN AGREEMENT IS ALLEGED IN PARAGRAPH TWENTY-FIVE. THE DAMAGES CLAIMED ARE READILY DISCERNIBLE FROM THE PLEADING. THE INTENTIONAL AND NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION CLAIMS ARE PLEAD WITH SUFFICIENT PARTICULARITY AND ALLEGE A REPRESENTATION AS TO A CURRENT, AS CONTRASTED WITH A FUTURE FACT, SPECIFICALLY THAT THE CONTRACT HAD BE SIGNED. THE DEMURRER OF 20/20 FASHIONS IS OFF CALENDAR IN LIGHT OF THE BANKRUPTCY STAY. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM A WILSON VS. PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 1, 2008 LINE 12. DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 7, 2008. THE COURT REQUIRES THAT BOTH PARTIES ARE TO PROVIDE, TO THE COURT, ANOTHER SET OF COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR PAPERS. WHEN SUBMITTING THE DUPLICATE COPIES, PLEASE PROVIDE A COVER LETTER TO THE COURT REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE SO THAT THE COURTESY COPIES WON'T BE MISPLACED WHEN RECEIVED. =(302/PJM)
